message: [drizzle-best-practices] Using `: any` type annotation on Drizzle query results defeats the type safety that Drizzle provides → Remove the `: any` annotation and let TypeScript infer the type from Drizzle, or use `typeof deals.$inferSelect[]` for explicit typing

[typescript-best-practices] Use unknown instead of any - using 'any' disables all type checking → Change 'const raw: any' to 'const raw: unknown' and narrow the type with schema.parse() result, or let the schema result be the typed value

[typescript-best-practices] Use unknown instead of any - using 'any' disables all type checking → Remove the explicit ': any' annotation and let TypeScript infer the type from the db query, or use a proper type definition

[drizzle-best-practices] Using generic Error instead of TRPCError in router loses structured error handling → Use throw new TRPCError({ code: 'NOT_FOUND', message: 'Contact not found' }) to maintain consistent error handling
[drizzle-best-practices] Using : any instead of : unknown or typed schema reduces type safety → Use : unknown and validate with schema.parse(), or type the result properly after validation
[typescript-best-practices] Using 'any' type annotation on variable declaration → Remove explicit ': any' and let TypeScript infer, or use ': unknown' with proper type guards

[typescript-best-practices] Use unknown instead of any - NEVER use any → Change `const contactDeals: any` to `const contactDeals = await db...` letting TypeScript infer the type, or use a proper type annotation

[next-best-practices] Inline script missing id attribute - per scripts.md, inline scripts require an id attribute for Next.js to track them → Add an id attribute to the inline script: <script id="theme-init" dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ ... }} />
